commit_scope: Execute chained member inequality guard returns in parsed BML BodySource for the thesis Rule.operator== method.

local_validation.notes: The chained-member proof executes if (m_hArgs.Count != hOther.Args.Count) return false; from original container-Rule.bml after the existing instanceof guard, hOther cast binding, and simple member mismatch guard.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A .bml method body can now compare a receiver/local member chain against another member chain and return false on mismatch in source order.
test_flows: python3 scripts/audit_bml_bmf_kernel_natives.py before commit to prove no new BML/BMF host-native semantics were introduced.

phase_gate.reason: Rule.operator== now supports chained member mismatch guards. Full thesis parity still requires the final equality return m_hItem == hOther.Item, loops, switch/select, exceptions, generic collections, and full BMF rule execution.

evidence_refs: form/form-stdlib/tests/bml-thesis-rule-operator-chain-member-compare-proof.fk proves the original Rule.operator== chained member mismatch guard against original container-Rule.bml.
